content.summary: Release certification should be debated around retained proof: real Codex expert host ids, runtime prompt-build artifacts, preserved raw host read batches, normalized transport collection, finalized WAL, trace, evidence, adapter-smoke, validate-loop, validate-discussion, and certify_adapter gates.

result.openQuestion: The main unresolved detail is the minimal manifest schema needed to bind every retained artifact to certification output without overburdening developers.
result.position: Certification should require retained artifacts proving real Codex host thread/expert transport and every runtime-owned gate before the adapter is marked release-certified.

content.summary: Do not certify the retained Codex adapter release unless real host transport and all runtime-produced artifacts and validation gates are retained and replayable.

result.openQuestion: Whether validate-loop is uniquely necessary as a certification blocker depends on whether it proves polling completeness and guards against partial host read collection.
result.position: The full retained chain in r1-msg-001 is justified for release certification, but the gate should distinguish between certification-critical evidence and supporting diagnostics.

content.summary: Do not certify on simulated or locally reconstructed transport; keep the retained real-host provenance chain as the release-smoke bar while making the P0 evidence explicit.

activeDisagreements: The skeptic would separate P0 certification blockers from P1 diagnostics and questions whether validate-loop is uniquely necessary if another gate proves polling completeness.
agreements: Both experts agree that release certification must not rely on simulated or locally reconstructed transport.

Both experts require runtime prompt-build artifacts, retained real Codex host ids, preserved raw host read batches, normalized transport collection, finalized WAL, trace/evidence, adapter-smoke, validate-discussion, and certify_adapter evidence before a certified release claim.
synthesis.recommendation: Treat the Codex adapter release as certified only when retained artifacts prove real Codex expert thread transport and all runtime-owned gates pass.
synthesis.recommendationRationale: The certifier argues that retained replayable artifacts turn certification from assertion into proof. The skeptic accepts the retained real-host chain as necessary for release-smoke certification because the core false positive is certifying mocked transport.
